books.title: Foundation
books.author: Isaac Asimov
books.elon_quote: I was trying to figure out whether there was a way to avoid the Dark Ages. It seemed like it might take a while, and then the Galactic Empire fell. Foundation is about trying to minimize that dark ages period.
books.description: A Hugo Award-winning classic about the fall of a galactic empire and one man's plan to preserve human knowledge across centuries.

books.title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
books.author: Douglas Adams
books.elon_quote: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy taught me that the universe is vast and largely indifferent to our problems — which is oddly comforting.
books.description: The comedic sci-fi masterpiece that follows Arthur Dent through the universe after Earth is demolished to make way for a hyperspace bypass.

books.title: Benjamin Franklin: An American Life
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.elon_quote: Franklin is one of my heroes. He was an entrepreneur, inventor, and statesman who fundamentally shaped America.
books.description: Walter Isaacson's definitive biography of the founding father who was a printer, inventor, diplomat, scientist, and self-made entrepreneur.

books.title: Einstein: His Life and Universe
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.elon_quote: Einstein's ability to visualize thought experiments and reason from first principles is something I deeply admire.
books.description: The first full biography of Albert Einstein written with complete access to his archives. A portrait of curiosity, creativity, and genius.

books.title: Structures: Or Why Things Don't Fall Down
books.author: J.E. Gordon
books.elon_quote: If you want to understand structural engineering, read Structures by J.E. Gordon. Fantastic book.
books.description: A brilliant, witty introduction to the principles of structural engineering — essential reading for anyone who wants to understand how things are built.

books.title: Zero to One
books.author: Peter Thiel
books.elon_quote: Zero to One is about how to build companies that create new things rather than competing in existing markets.
books.description: Peter Thiel's contrarian guide to building startups that create genuinely new things rather than copying what works.

books.title: The Moon Is a Harsh Mistress
books.author: Robert A. Heinlein
books.elon_quote: Heinlein's work instilled in me the dream of humanity becoming a spacefaring civilization.
books.description: A Hugo Award winner about a lunar colony's revolution against Earth — Heinlein at his most libertarian and most thrilling.

books.title: Superintelligence: Paths, Dangers, Strategies
books.author: Nick Bostrom
books.elon_quote: Worth reading Superintelligence by Bostrom. We need to be super careful with AI. It is potentially more dangerous than nukes.
books.description: The landmark book exploring what happens when machine intelligence surpasses human intelligence — and what we need to do about it.

books.title: Life of Pi
books.author: Yann Martel
books.elon_quote: A beautiful meditation on survival, belief, and the stories we tell ourselves.
books.description: The Man Booker Prize-winning novel about a boy stranded on a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger — one of the most extraordinary survival stories ever told.

books.title: The Lord of the Rings
books.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
books.elon_quote: The heroes of the books I read always felt a duty to save the world. That's been a deep influence on me.
books.description: The defining work of high fantasy — the epic quest to destroy the One Ring and save Middle-earth from darkness.

books.title: Catherine the Great: Portrait of a Woman
books.author: Robert K. Massie
books.elon_quote: An incredible story of someone who came from nothing and through sheer will transformed an empire.
books.description: Pulitzer Prize-winning biography of Russia's most powerful female ruler — a woman who seized power and transformed a nation.

books.title: The Idiot
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.elon_quote: Dostoevsky understood the human condition at a depth few writers have ever matched.
books.description: Dostoevsky's masterpiece about a kind-hearted Russian prince whose goodness is no match for the corrupt society he enters.

books.title: Les Misérables
books.author: Victor Hugo
books.elon_quote: Les Misérables is the story of someone who refused to accept the world as it was and fought to make it better.
books.description: Victor Hugo's monumental novel of justice, redemption, and revolution in 19th-century France — one of the greatest novels ever written.

books.title: Howard Hughes: His Life and Madness
books.author: Donald L. Barlett & James B. Steele
books.elon_quote: Howard Hughes was a cautionary tale and an inspiration — a man who pushed boundaries but was ultimately consumed by his obsessions.
books.description: The definitive biography of the aviator, filmmaker, and industrialist who became the most famous recluse of the 20th century.

books.title: The Innovators
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.elon_quote: The story of the digital revolution is the story of collaboration — it was never just one genius working alone.
books.description: How the digital revolution came to be — the creative entrepreneurs and hackers who made the computer and internet revolution.

books.title: Merchants of Doubt
books.author: Naomi Oreskes & Erik M. Conway
books.elon_quote: Understanding how doubt is manufactured about scientific consensus is essential for anyone trying to make the world better.
books.description: How a small group of scientists and their political allies deceived the public on issues from tobacco to climate change.

books.title: The Rocket Men
books.author: Rex Hall & David Shayler
books.elon_quote: Understanding the early history of human spaceflight is essential context for where we need to go next.
books.description: The definitive history of Soviet cosmonauts and the space race that defined a generation.

books.title: Ignition!
books.author: John D. Clark
books.elon_quote: Ignition is a fascinating account of the people who developed rocket propellants — it reads like a thriller.
books.description: An informal history of liquid rocket propellants — a cult classic among rocket engineers, republished after decades out of print.

books.title: The Big Picture
books.author: Sean Carroll
books.elon_quote: Sean Carroll is one of the best at explaining the fundamental nature of reality in accessible terms.
books.description: A physicist's exploration of the origins of life, meaning, and the universe itself — from the Big Bang to human consciousness.

books.title: Surely You're Joking, Mr. Feynman!
books.author: Richard Feynman
books.elon_quote: Feynman embodied the spirit of curiosity and first-principles thinking better than almost anyone.
books.description: The wildly entertaining memoir of Nobel Prize-winning physicist Richard Feynman — adventurer, safe-cracker, bongo player, and genius.

books.title: The Culture Series
books.author: Iain M. Banks
books.elon_quote: The Culture series is the most positive depiction of an advanced technological future I've encountered.
books.description: Banks's epic space opera series set in a post-scarcity interstellar civilization — the benchmark for optimistic science fiction.

books.title: Our Final Invention
books.author: James Barrat
books.elon_quote: Understanding the risks of artificial superintelligence is one of the most important things we can do right now.
books.description: A journalist's investigation into the dangers of artificial general intelligence and what leading scientists think we should do about it.

books.title: The Art of War
books.author: Sun Tzu
books.elon_quote: Strategy, timing, and knowing when not to fight — lessons that apply far beyond the battlefield.
books.description: The ancient Chinese military treatise that remains one of the most influential texts on strategy, leadership, and conflict.

books.title: Steve Jobs
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.elon_quote: Steve showed that design and engineering are not opposites — they reinforce each other when you refuse to compromise.
books.description: The definitive biography of Apple's co-founder — written with exclusive access and based on more than forty interviews.

books.title: Elon Musk
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.elon_quote: Walter spent two years following me around. I don't agree with everything in it, but it's a serious attempt to understand what drives me.
books.description: Walter Isaacson's authorized biography of Elon Musk — two years in the making, with unprecedented access to the man himself.
